hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (818) landed well below the Top 100 average (1,269), and overheal (53.1%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (58%, a 4.9-point gap) - both real gaps despite a strong 79th-percentile result, bolstered by ranking 1st of 4 tracked healers on both real comparison metrics this kill.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 17.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate composition gap, on a kill where all healing landed on just 3 targets.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every cooldown here tracked the Top 100 sample except Rebirth, cast on Zamnes about 34 seconds after Zamnes's own death - a real overcast against a norm where only 10% of parses use it here, but a genuine, if delayed, save.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (100% vs. 83.3% average, +16.7) and top-target concentration (72.7% vs. 47.9% average, +24.8) both ran well above the Top 100 norm - all of this kill's healing landed on just 3 real targets (Tauntt, Zamnes, Turbozool), despite 10 real raid deaths logged overall.
hydross.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 12.9% of the fight under 20%), ending around 86.8%. A second Mana Potion use would have been available before the kill ended based on the first use's timing, but wasn't taken.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (855) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,605) - the weakest HPS ratio to its own benchmark of any kill this raid night (53%) - and overheal (53.3%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (49.1%), despite only 2 real deaths logged, still landing at the 63rd percentile.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 12.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the smallest positive Lifebloom gap of the raid night, though still a real overcast.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went uncast against a 72%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap on this kill. Neither of the 2 real deaths (Dylfarian, Dodohpih) shows a cooldown landing close enough afterward to call a real save - the nearest timings (20.7 and 55.8 seconds out) are too far removed.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (69.8% vs. 56.8% average, +13.0) ran above the Top 100 norm, with 20 distinct targets touched - the widest target spread on a below-median-HPS kill this raid night, consistent with a kill that still overhealed more than the whole Top 100 sample despite reaching most of the raid.
lurker.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 18.6% of this kill below 20% mana (26 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (773) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,517), and overheal (63.3%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (54.3%) by 9 points - the largest overheal gap of the raid night - with 8 real deaths logged (tied for the most of any kill this raid night), landing at the 57th percentile.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 48.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the second-largest composition gap of the raid night, on the same kill that carried the raid's worst overheal gap.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown matched the Top 100 sample in count, but none of the 8 real deaths show a clean save - the closest timing (Unstraight, 1.0 second after a Swiftmend cast) suggests the heal simply wasn't enough, not a missed cast. No Rebirth was attempted despite 8 deaths on a kill that ultimately succeeded, meaning real time remained afterward - a real, plausible missed opportunity.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (86.5% vs. 51% average, +35.5) ran far above the Top 100 norm - the largest coverage gap of the raid night - with 15 distinct targets touched, consistent with a chaotic, high-death fight.
leotheras.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 9.2% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 1.6% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one. A second Mana Potion use would have been available before the kill ended based on the first use's timing, but wasn't taken.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,314) ran below the Top 100 average (1,834), and overheal (49.3%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (46.5%) - with 5 real deaths logged, still a strong 87th-percentile kill overall.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 44.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the largest composition gaps of the raid night.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Both Nature's Swiftness (76%-used norm) and Swiftmend (93%-used norm, the most heavily-used cooldown in the whole Top 100 sample) went completely uncast this kill - a real, notable gap given how close to universal Swiftmend usage is in the benchmark. None of the 5 real deaths land near a healing-relevant cooldown; the closest timings are all self-targeted Mana Potion or Innervate casts, not saves.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (91.6% vs. 74% average, +17.6) and top-target concentration (45% vs. 34.5% average, +10.5) both ran above the Top 100 norm, across 12 distinct targets.
karathress.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 8.3% of the fight under 20%), ending around 47.1%. A second Mana Potion use would have been available before the kill ended based on the first use's timing, but wasn't taken.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,163) ran modestly below the Top 100 average (1,442, an 81% ratio - the closest to average of any kill this raid night), with overheal (41.8%) comfortably inside the Top 100 range (23.7%-51.1%) - a genuinely clean, in-line kill at the 95th percentile.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 23.4 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap, in an otherwise textbook-average kill.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Érel just 3.9 seconds after Érel's death - the tightest, cleanest death-to-Rebirth turnaround of the whole raid night, a real, clear save. Every other tracked cooldown matched the Top 100 sample.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: 22 distinct targets touched - the widest spread of any kill this raid night - with coverage (67.5% vs. 67.4% average) and top-target concentration (38.7% vs. 38.5% average) both landing almost exactly on the Top 100 norm - the most evenly-distributed healing profile of the night.
morogrim.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 7.2% of the fight under 20%), ending around 27.1%.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (933) ran below the Top 100 average (1,139), with overheal (45.9%) comfortably inside the Top 100 range (34.3%-53.5%) - a strong 95th-percentile result on the raid's longest kill (581 seconds) and heaviest death count (10, including Danceswtrees's own).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran only 8.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the smallest positive Lifebloom gap of the raid night, tracking closer to the benchmark than any other kill.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Largebingus about 2.6 minutes after Largebingus's first death - a real, delayed but plausible save, though Largebingus died again later in a cluster of 5 deaths within a single second near the end of the fight. Swiftmend was used 7 times (the heaviest use of the raid night) and Mana Potion 4 times, both in line with this fight's long, mana-intensive Top 100 norm. Danceswtrees's own death on this kill isn't explained by any nearby cooldown - the nearest logged cast (a Swiftmend, 43.4 seconds prior) isn't close enough to call a factor.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: 21 distinct targets touched - the second-widest spread of the raid night - with coverage (66.6% vs. 50.7% average, +15.9) and top-target concentration (44.9% vs. 21% average, +23.9) both well above the Top 100 norm.
vashj.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 11.7% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 0.6% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (831) ran below the Top 100 average (1,075), and overheal (56.3%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (61.1%, a 4.8-point gap) - yet this was still a strong 94th-percentile kill, with only 2 real deaths logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 20.4 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap, consistent with the raid night's broader pattern.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast on Zamnes just 3.8 seconds after Zamnes's death - the second-tightest death-to-Rebirth turnaround of the raid night (behind only Morogrim's), a real, clean save. Every other tracked cooldown matched the Top 100 sample.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (93.2% vs. 78.9% average, +14.3) ran above the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (46.2% vs. 43.5% average, +2.7) tracked closer to typical than on any other kill this raid night - the most evenly-distributed top-target share of the night.
alar.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed comfortable for most of the kill (only 9.5% of the fight under 20%) but was driven down to 2.7% by the end - a late squeeze, not a sustained one.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,385) ran well below the Top 100 average (2,458, a 56% ratio - the second-weakest of the raid night), and overheal (39.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (33%) by 6.4 points - with 6 real deaths logged, landing at only the 56th percentile.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's share ran 65.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average - by far the largest composition gap of the entire raid night.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldown deviated in count from the Top 100 sample, but none of the 6 real deaths show a genuine save either - the closest timings (Swiftmend, 7.5-12.2 seconds out) can't be confirmed as landing on the players who actually died, and the Mana Potion/Innervate proximity is purely self-cast coincidence, not a save attempt.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (26.3% vs. 9.4% average, +16.9) and coverage (83.1% vs. 37.2% average, +45.9) both ran far above the Top 100 norm - the largest coverage gap of the entire raid night by a wide margin - across 13 distinct targets.
voidreaver.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Spent 42.1% of this kill below 20% mana (60 casts made that low), a real efficiency squeeze rather than a one-off dip.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (541) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,225, a 44% ratio - the weakest of the entire raid night), and overheal (67.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (65.4%) - the highest raw overheal percentage of the raid night - yet this still landed at the 66th percentile on the raid's shortest kill (165 seconds), with only 2 real deaths logged.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation's share ran 8.5 points behind the Top 100 average - the only kill this raid night where the top composition gap runs opposite Danceswtrees's usual Lifebloom-heavy pattern.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness (69%-used norm) and Mana Potion (61%-used norm) both went uncast this kill - a real gap that lines up with this boss's own weakest mana-efficiency ratio (63% of the Top 100 average HPM) and lowest active time of the raid night (93.4%). Neither of the 2 real deaths lands near a healing-relevant cooldown - both show only a self-cast Innervate 15-40 seconds out.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (66.4% vs. 52.8% average, +13.6) and top-target concentration (33.9% vs. 16.4% average, +17.5) both ran above the Top 100 norm, across 20 distinct targets.
solarian.MANA_TIMING_FINDING: Mana stayed out of real danger all kill (only 12.4% of the fight under 20%), ending around 89.5%.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: one real mid-raid gear change (Feet), plus one likely-benign single-kill gear swap (MainHand/OffHand)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Feet slot (item 30092) carried no enchant on the first 6 kills (Hydross through Vashj), then picked up a permanent enchant partway through the raid, present on the final 3 kills (Alar, Void Reaver, Solarian) - a real, fixed gear gap, not a benign variant. The Back slot (item 28765) carries no permanent enchant on all 9 kills - a second, persistent gap distinct from the Feet-slot fix.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Kael'thas Sunstrider was attempted 3 times and never killed - the raid's only non-kill this night, and not covered by any boss page here. Across the 9 real kills, Danceswtrees ranked 1st of 4 tracked healers by both real comparison metrics (item-level and raw healing rank) on 6 of 9 kills, with a 77% average item-level rank percentile - a strong raid night by internal comparison. Raw HPS itself trailed the Top 100 average on every single kill (44%-96% of the benchmark average), while target coverage and top-target concentration ran above the Top 100 norm on every kill, and Lifebloom's composition share ran ahead of the Top 100 average on 8 of 9 kills (Solarian was the exception, running behind on Rejuvenation instead) - a consistent pattern of concentrated, priority-target Lifebloom stacking. Overheal was at or above the Top 100 sample's own single worst parse on 5 of 9 kills (Lurker, Leotheras, Karathress, Void Reaver, Solarian) and within 5 points of it on 2 more (Hydross, Alar) - only Morogrim and Vashj landed solidly in line with the benchmark. Real deaths were heavy this raid night (10 on Hydross, 10 on Vashj including Danceswtrees's own single death, 8 on Leotheras, 6 on Void Reaver, 5 on Karathress), despite the strong internal rankings.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample's own single worst parse on 5 of 9 kills this raid night, with 2 more within 5 points of it. Combined with a Lifebloom composition share running ahead of the Top 100 average on 8 of 9 kills, this points to a consistent priority-target HoT-stacking pattern worth a rotation review - see each boss's own page for the real numbers.

GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Boots (item 30092) carried no permanent enchant on Hydross through Vashj, then picked up enchant 2940 starting on Alar through the rest of the raid - a real, mid-raid gear fix, not a lasting gap.

GearCheckItems.Description: MainHand and OffHand vary on a single kill
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Both slots swap to a fishing pole and lure on The Lurker Below only, consistent with a fight-specific tactical choice for that encounter, not a gearing gap.

GearCheckItems.Description: Elixir of Healing Power (Battle) and Elixir of Draenic Wisdom (Guardian) both active on all 9 kills, plus Well Fed food buff
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, complete consumable setup across the whole raid night - the Battle + Guardian Elixir pairing is a fully valid alternative to a Flask under the real TBC rule.
